activities_projects.title: Understanding the Constitution
activities_projects.objective: Understand the meaning and importance of a constitution
steps: Read the definition of constitution from the textbook
Write the meaning in your own words
List two reasons why a country needs a constitution
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the basic concept of a constitution
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of explanation and correct points (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Poster on Nelson Mandela
activities_projects.objective: Learn about the role of Nelson Mandela in South Africa
steps: Collect information about Nelson Mandela
Prepare a poster describing his struggle against apartheid
Write 3–4 key achievements
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand Mandela’s contribution to democracy

activities_projects.title: Meaning of Apartheid
steps: Read about apartheid in the textbook
Write the meaning of apartheid
List two effects of apartheid on people
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand racial discrimination in South Africa

activities_projects.title: Preamble Reading Activity
activities_projects.objective: Understand the Preamble of the Indian Constitution
steps: Read the Preamble aloud in class
Underline important words such as justice, liberty and equality
Write the meaning of any two terms
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students become familiar with the values of the Constitution

activities_projects.title: Values of the Constitution Chart
activities_projects.objective: Learn the guiding values of the Constitution
steps: List key values like Sovereign, Socialist, Secular, Democratic and Republic
Write the meaning of each value
Decorate the chart with symbols
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct explanation and neat presentation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Group Discussion on Democracy
steps: Form small groups in the class
Discuss why democracy needs a constitution
Write two important ideas from the discussion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn democratic thinking and discussion skills

activities_projects.title: Timeline of Indian Constitution
activities_projects.objective: Understand the timeline of constitution making
steps: Write important dates like 1946, 1949 and 1950
Explain what happened on each date
Draw a simple timeline
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the process of constitution making

activities_projects.title: Leaders of Constituent Assembly
activities_projects.objective: Identify important leaders involved in drafting the Constitution
steps: Find names of leaders such as B.R. Ambedkar and Rajendra Prasad
Write one contribution of each leader
Present the information in class

activities_projects.title: Comparing Constitutions
steps: Read the preambles of India and South Africa
Write two similarities between them
Write one difference
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop comparative understanding of constitutions

activities_projects.title: Role Play on Constituent Assembly
activities_projects.objective: Understand how the Constitution was debated and framed
steps: Assign students roles of leaders like Ambedkar and Nehru
Discuss ideas about equality and rights
Act out a short debate in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand democratic discussion and decision making
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and understanding of roles (10 marks)
